plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-590 RP19.P03.M06.S07-RP19.P04.M05.S07 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-590 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP19 p03 closeout p04 foundation: the P03 Synthetic Fixture Set tail (the fourteen remaining rows), the full P03 Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles, the full P04 Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, Primary Implementation Slice, and Secondary Workflow Slice cycles, and the P04 Permission And Audit Binding head (the first seven rows) as eleven descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H19), Claude review packet (C19), and CP00-591 handoff, without opening client portal, secure link, client review, or watermark runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-590 is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the RP19 Client Portal Core program covering exactly 150 planned units (RP19.P03.M06.S07–RP19.P04.M05.S07, risk class C). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack's claims are supported and no defects were found. The production_ready claim is correctly NEGATIVE (production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:descriptor) — no runtime overclaim and no weakening of production_ready gates; the only positive signal is production_ready_candidate, which is correctly gated on coverage.valid && slice.valid. Descriptor-only boundaries are actively ENFORCED in validators (per-row descriptor_only===true, runtime_execution===false, customer_safe_errors_only===true, raw_payload_included===false), not merely asserted, with no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, and synthetic_only:true. The Claude authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, with Claude as read_only_independent_reviewer and not final approver across service.js, validators.js, and manifest.json. Hermes appears only as deterministic H19 descriptor-evidence handling (no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true), with no product runtime embedded. Coverage reconciles to 150 units and the contract JSON deep-equals the JS-generated descriptors via the RP19 validator with no drift. Review-hardening (CP178+) preconditions pass: the auth preflight succeeded with non-zero (1456-byte) valid JSON (is_error:false), so the evidence is not an auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced-prose, or tool-call-shaped output; tools were restricted to Read/Grep/Glob with forbidden_tools_present:false and summary_only_review disallowed (Risk C + security/audit + review-evidence deliverables present). Post-review artifacts (raw-output, review-receipt, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) are correctly absent as the expected pre-review state, not a gap. Verdict: PASS — pack closeout may proceed to the human/final approval step that closes the enterprise-trust claim outside this receipt.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-590 client portal core p03 closeout p04 foundation descriptor pack
